subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – A Funny Man
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: In a quiet little village, there lived a man named Mr. Bumble. He was known as a very funny man because he did everything in a strange way. He always wore a bright yellow hat and shoes that squeaked with every step.
questions: What was the man's name?
Where did he live?
Why was he known as a funny man?
What color was his hat?
What sound did his shoes make?

passages.paragraph: Mr. Bumble did not like to do things the normal way. Every morning, he ate his breakfast under the kitchen table instead of sitting on a chair. He said the toast tasted much better when eaten on the floor.
questions: Did Mr. Bumble like doing things normally?
Where did he eat his breakfast?
What did he not sit on?
What food did he mention?
Why did he eat on the floor?

passages.paragraph: When it rained, Mr. Bumble never used an umbrella to stay dry. Instead, he would carry a large frying pan over his head. The children in the village would laugh as the raindrops made a 'ping' sound on the metal.
questions: What did Mr. Bumble not use when it rained?
What did he carry over his head instead?
Who laughed at him?
What sound did the raindrops make?
What was the 'umbrella' made of?

passages.paragraph: Mr. Bumble had a very unusual pet. It was not a dog or a cat, but a large green frog named Flippy. He taught Flippy how to sit on his shoulder and wear a tiny red bowtie.
questions: What kind of pet did Mr. Bumble have?
What was the pet's name?
What color was the pet?
Where did the pet sit?
What did the pet wear?

passages.paragraph: One afternoon, Mr. Bumble decided to paint his front door. Instead of using a brush, he used a bunch of long feathers. He painted the door with purple dots and orange stripes, making it the brightest house on the street.
questions: What did Mr. Bumble decide to do one afternoon?
What did he use instead of a brush?
What two patterns did he paint on the door?
What colors did he use?
How did his house look compared to others?

passages.paragraph: Mr. Bumble loved to go for walks, but he never walked forward. He always walked backward so he could see where he had been. People often bumped into him, but he just tipped his hat and smiled.
questions: How did Mr. Bumble walk?
Why did he walk that way?
Did he like going for walks?
What happened when people bumped into him?
What did he do with his hat when he smiled?

passages.paragraph: For his birthday, Mr. Bumble bought himself a cake made entirely of mashed potatoes. He put blue candles on top and sang 'Happy Birthday' to himself in a very high, squeaky voice.
questions: What was the cake made of?
What color were the candles?
What song did he sing?
What kind of voice did he use?
Whose birthday was it?

passages.paragraph: The neighbors once saw Mr. Bumble trying to water his garden with a teapot. He told them that flowers prefer a nice cup of water just like people prefer tea. The flowers seemed to grow very tall and happy.
questions: What did he use to water his garden?
Who saw him doing this?
What did he say the flowers preferred?
What do people prefer according to Mr. Bumble?
How did the flowers grow?

passages.paragraph: Mr. Bumble’s clock was also very funny. It did not have numbers; it had pictures of different fruits. When the big hand pointed to the banana, he knew it was time for his afternoon nap.
questions: What was special about his clock?
What was on the clock instead of numbers?
Which fruit showed it was nap time?
What part of the clock pointed to the fruit?
What did he do when the hand hit the banana?

passages.paragraph: Everyone in the village loved the funny man. He taught them that it is okay to be different and that laughter is the best medicine. Even though he was strange, he was the kindest person they knew.
questions: How did the village feel about Mr. Bumble?
What was the first lesson he taught them?
What did he say was the best medicine?
Was he a mean person?
Was it okay to be different?
